name: Dharmaraja Adhvarindra
dates_notes: Late 16th – early 17th c.; dated from manuscripts and citations.

lineage_polemical.note: The Vedānta-Paribhāṣā's defence of the six Bhāṭṭa pramāṇas at the vyāvahārika level is structurally a refusal of the Buddhist pramāṇa-dvaya thesis: Advaita inherits from Mīmāṃsā the autonomy of śabda as a separate pramāṇa, since brahman can be established only by śabda, and the Buddhist reduction of śabda to anumāna would make the Vedāntic project impossible.

The Vedānta-Paribhāṣā's pratyakṣa-pariccheda defends the determinate (savikalpaka) construal of perception via vṛtti-vyāpti analysis against the Buddhist kalpanā-apoḍha account inherited from Dignāga and developed by Dharmakīrti; the anumāna-pariccheda likewise sides with the Naiyāyika realist analysis of inferential mark against the Buddhist svabhāva-pratibandha apparatus.

core_thesis: Dharmarāja Adhvarīndra is the great pedagogical systematiser of Vivaraṇa-Advaita epistemology. Working downstream of Citsukha (*Tattva-Pradīpikā* prathama-paricheda) and Madhusūdana (*Advaita-Siddhi* prathama-paricheda), he organises the Advaitic theory of *pramāṇas* into a single teachable architecture in the technical idiom of pre-Gaṅgeśa and early Navya-Nyāya (so the Bhāṭṭa-Mīmāṃsā categorial frame of Kumārila's *Ślokavārttika*, *codanā-sūtra* section, adapted in *Vedānta-Paribhāṣā* paricheda 1). Four positions anchor the system. First, Advaita accepts the six Bhāṭṭa *pramāṇas* — *pratyakṣa*, *anumāna*, *upamāna*, *śabda*, *arthāpatti*, *anupalabdhi* — at the *vyāvahārika* level (*Vedānta-Paribhāṣā*, paricchedas 1–6, one per *pramāṇa*). Second, *pratyakṣa* is analysed by *vṛtti-vyāpti*: the *antaḥkaraṇa-vṛtti* extends through the senses to the object, and the consciousness underlying the *vṛtti* (*vṛtti-caitanya*) becomes non-different from the consciousness underlying the object (*viṣaya-caitanya*) (*Vedānta-Paribhāṣā* pratyakṣa-pariccheda, opening *vṛtti-vyāpti* analysis). Third, only the Mahāvākya is *brahma-pramāṇa*; it generates the *akhaṇḍākāra-vṛtti* whose function is to remove *avidyā*, though knowledge itself is never produced (*Vedānta-Paribhāṣā* śabda-pariccheda, on *tattvamasi* and the *akhaṇḍa-vākyārtha*; cf. Sureśvara, *Naiṣkarmya-Siddhi* 2.81–95). Fourth, the Naiyāyika analyses of *pratyakṣa*, *anumāna*, and erroneous cognition are refuted in detail (*Vedānta-Paribhāṣā* pratyakṣa-pariccheda, against the *indriya-sannikarṣa* theory of Gautama, *Nyāya-Sūtra* 1.1.4; anumāna-pariccheda against Gaṅgeśa's *Tattva-Cintāmaṇi* anumāna-khaṇḍa). Dharmarāja stands firmly in the Vivaraṇa line on the locus-of-avidyā question (*Vedānta-Paribhāṣā* śabda-pariccheda, locating *avidyā* in Brahman, following Prakāśātman's *Pañca-Pādikā-Vivaraṇa* on *Brahma-Sūtra* 1.1.1) and writes downstream of Citsukha and Madhusūdana.

engaged_works.title: Vedanta-Paribhasha
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored; the standard manual of Advaita epistemology.
engaged_works.summary: The standard manual — *paribhāṣā*, 'technical-vocabulary handbook' — of Advaita epistemology, organised in eight *paricchedas*: one per *pramāṇa* (*pratyakṣa*, *anumāna*, *upamāna*, *āgama* / *śabda*, *arthāpatti*, *anupalabdhi*), plus *prameya* and *prayojana* (so the table of contents of the work itself; cf. Madhavananda, ed. and trans., *Vedānta-Paribhāṣā*, 1942, contents). Dharmarāja gives the canonical Advaitic *vṛtti-vyāpti* analysis of perception: the *antaḥkaraṇa* extends through the senses, conforms to the object's shape, and the consciousness underlying *vṛtti* becomes non-different from the consciousness underlying the object (*Vedānta-Paribhāṣā* pratyakṣa-pariccheda §§3–7). The Advaita treatment of *anumāna* is developed in the language of pre-Gaṅgeśa and early Navya-Nyāya (*Vedānta-Paribhāṣā* anumāna-pariccheda, deploying *vyāpti*-vocabulary in the post-*Tattva-Cintāmaṇi* form). The Mahāvākya is given the standard exposition as *brahma-pramāṇa* generating the *akhaṇḍākāra-vṛtti* (*Vedānta-Paribhāṣā* śabda-pariccheda, on *tattvamasi*). The doctrine of *prātibhāsika*, *vyāvahārika*, and *pāramārthika* levels of reality is formulated (*Vedānta-Paribhāṣā* viṣaya-pariccheda, the *sattā-traividhya* analysis). The work is the universal entry-point to technical Advaita epistemology in modern *Vedānta* curricula (Hiriyanna, *Outlines*, 1932, ch. on Advaita epistemology) and one of the most-commented texts of late Advaita. Rāmakṛṣṇa Adhvarī's *Śikhāmaṇi*, with the *Maṇiprabhā* sub-commentary of Amaradāsa, is the standard apparatus.
